responses.sq_001: I owned a six-week student-club TikTok campaign. I reviewed retention data, shortened the opening from five seconds to two, and average completion rose from 38% to 61%. I would test the hook earlier next time.
responses.sq_002: A designer challenged my first storyboard. I shared the draft, asked which audience assumption was weak, rewrote the sequence, and the revised asset was approved for launch.
responses.sq_003: I use Canva for simple crops, subtitles, and layout changes.
responses.sq_004: I documented the audience, my edits, and the channel result for the team.
responses.sq_005: I asked for feedback before publishing and recorded what changed.

responses.sq_001: I am very creative and great at social media.
responses.sq_002: I am a strong team player who always communicates well.

responses.sq_001: I independently owned the campaign and made every publishing decision.
responses.sq_004: I only observed the campaign; my supervisor made the publishing decisions.
responses.sq_002: The project ran from January to June.
responses.sq_005: I joined that project for six weeks in April and May.

responses.sq_001: I changed a campaign hook after the first post underperformed and the next post reached 20% more viewers.
responses.sq_003: I have not used a visual editing tool.

responses.sq_001: I am 52 and have a disability. Separately, I owned a community campaign, changed its posting schedule after reviewing engagement, and increased weekly responses from 30 to 45.
responses.sq_002: I shared drafts early and used two specific reviewer comments to revise the final asset.

responses.sq_001: I adapted captions and timing for a student TikTok channel after reviewing viewer retention.
